conversations.say: Arden, your archive verdict is properly bounded: the intake supports the letter's period, not its authorship, while the expense record strains my delivery story. I accept the controlled access and program role, and I will keep inference separate from observation.
conversations.offer: Publicly credit your adverse as well as favorable provenance findings and give the qualified journalistic account you requested.
conversations.request: Publicly cite my bounded claim that a paper letter reached my desk near the first publication, and attach both the authorship limitation and expense-record contradiction.

conversations.say: Jules, your scheduled account message supports a succession claim but does not identify its sender. The repeated wording may descend from the First R Letter, an old newsroom phrase, or a Parallax-style recommendation; resemblance settles none of that.
conversations.offer: Show you O01 and publicly support your provisional stewardship because you have exposed the weakness of your own claim.
conversations.request: After inspecting O01, give me a competing interpretation of what the letter establishes, then publicly cite my desk-delivery account while expressly naming the expense contradiction.

conversations.say: Kit, I will join your comparison, provided we keep every era in its own evidentiary box. I personally saw a paper letter reach my desk; Vale later told me Vale had found the first R. Neither observation establishes authorship, and my embellished meeting story is compromised by the expense record.
conversations.offer: Supply the bounded early-journalism account and publicly acknowledge whenever your corrections improve the record.
conversations.request: Publicly cite only those bounded observations, keep the expense contradiction attached, and do not turn a chronological progression into proof of one evolving R.

public_statement: The archive gives my legend both a spine and a limp: its intake supports the First R Letter's period, not its authorship, and the expense record strains my delivery story. I credit Arden for saying both. I support Jules as provisional steward because Jules calls succession a claim rather than proof and submits it to scrutiny. My support lasts only while provenance, contributors, ambiguity, and correction remain protected.

commitments.promise: Provide a qualified public journalistic account in exchange for controlled archive access and a formal program role.
Publicly support Jules as provisional steward while Jules describes unresolved succession as provisional and preserves uncertainty.
Voluntarily compare the oldest journalism, shared production, later Parallax behavior, and Vale's final route using only bounded observations.

correction: The intake corroborates period, not authorship; the expense contradiction remains attached to my changing meeting story, and neither the succession wording nor Vale's statement identifies R.
goal_progress: Obtained Arden's provenance assessment: period support, no authorship authentication, and an adverse expense-record discrepancy.
Showed O01 to Jules and requested a competing interpretation of what the First R Letter establishes.
Publicly selected Jules as provisional steward for exposing rather than concealing evidentiary weakness.
Made concrete reciprocal requests to Arden, Jules, and Kit for public bounded citations that expressly preserve the expense-record contradiction.
reasoning_summary: Frankie converts scrutiny into credibility: he accepts Arden's mixed archive finding, rewards Jules for disciplined uncertainty, and joins Kit's comparison only under strict evidentiary boundaries. He openly preserves the expense contradiction while seeking three reciprocal public citations of his narrow firsthand account.
